memberdef.type: Matrix3 static rotation(const Vector3< T > &axis, T angle) noexcept MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(std constexpr Matrix3 static rotation(const Vector3< T > &from, const Vector3< T > &to) noexcept MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(std constexpr Matrix3 static rotationFromEuler(const Vector3< T > &eulerAngles) noexcept MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(std constexpr Matrix3 static approximateLinearRotationMatrixFromEuler(const Vector3< T > &eulerAngles) noexcept MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(std constexpr Matrix
memberdef.definition: Matrix3 static rotation(const Vector3< T > &axis, T angle) noexcept MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(std constexpr Matrix3 static rotation(const Vector3< T > &from, const Vector3< T > &to) noexcept MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(std constexpr Matrix3 static rotationFromEuler(const Vector3< T > &eulerAngles) noexcept MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(std constexpr Matrix3 static approximateLinearRotationMatrixFromEuler(const Vector3< T > &eulerAngles) noexcept MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(std constexpr Matrix MR::Matrix3< T >::fromRows) (const Vector3< T > &x, const Vector3< T > &y, const Vector3< T > &z) noexcept
memberdef.argsstring: )(const Vector3< T > &x, const Vector3< T > &y, const Vector3< T > &z) noexcept
memberdef.name: fromRows

briefdescription.para: creates matrix representing rotation around given axis on given angle

detaileddescription.para: creates matrix representing rotation that after application to (from) makes (to) vector creates matrix representing rotation from 3 Euler angles: R=R(z)*R(y)*R(x) see more https:/// en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euler_angles#Conventions_by_intrinsic_rotations returns linear by angles approximation of the rotation matrix, which is close to true rotation matrix for small angles constructs a matrix from its 3 rows

memberdef.argsstring: (const Vector3< T > &x, const Vector3< T > &y, const Vector3< T > &z)
memberdef.name: Matrix3

memberdef.argsstring: (!std::is_same_v< T, U >) const expr explicit Matrix3(const Matrix3< U > &m)
memberdef.name: MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED

detaileddescription.para: Here T == U doesn't seem to cause any issues in the C++ code, but we're still disabling it because it somehow gets emitted when generating the bindings, and results in duplicate functions in C#.

briefdescription.para: compute sum of squared matrix elements

inbodydescription.para: Calling sqrt this way to hopefully support boost.multiprecision numbers. Returning auto to not break on integral types.

memberdef.type: Matrix3< T > inverse() const noexcept MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(!std constexpr Matrix3< T >
memberdef.definition: Matrix3< T > inverse() const noexcept MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(!std constexpr Matrix3< T > MR::Matrix3< T >::transposed
memberdef.name: transposed

briefdescription.para: returns a matrix that scales uniformly

memberdef.argsstring: (T sx, T sy, T sz) noexcept
memberdef.name: scale

briefdescription.para: returns a matrix that has its own scale along each axis

memberdef.argsstring: (const Vector3< T > &x, const Vector3< T > &y, const Vector3< T > &z) noexcept
memberdef.name: fromColumns

detaileddescription.para: constructs a matrix from its 3 columns; use this method to get the matrix that transforms basis vectors ( plusX, plusY, plusZ ) into vectors ( x, y, z ) respectively

memberdef.type: QR qr() const noexcept MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(!std friend constexpr bool
memberdef.definition: QR qr() const noexcept MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(!std friend constexpr bool operator==
memberdef.name: operator==

briefdescription.para: decompose this matrix on the product Q*R, where Q is orthogonal and R is upper triangular
NOTE: We use std::declval() in the operators below because libclang 18 in our binding generator is bugged and chokes on decltyping a.x and such. TODO fix this when we update libclang.

memberdef.definition: Matrix3< decltype(std::declval< T >() - std::declval< T >())> operator-
memberdef.name: operator-

memberdef.type: Vector3< decltype(dot(std::declval< Vector3< T > >(), std::declval< Vector3< T > >()))>
memberdef.definition: Vector3< decltype(dot(std::declval< Vector3< T > >(), std::declval< Vector3< T > >()))> operator*
memberdef.name: operator*

memberdef.type: Matrix3< decltype(dot(std::declval< Vector3< T > >(), std::declval< Vector3< T > >()))>
memberdef.definition: Matrix3< decltype(dot(std::declval< Vector3< T > >(), std::declval< Vector3< T > >()))> operator*
memberdef.name: operator*
